title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control
description: A release is triggered from an unreviewed tag, rebuilds after approval, publishes npm with a long-lived token, mixes changelog generation with mutation, and cannot prove package or image provenance. Learners cumulatively produce multi-artifact release and publication policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
